name: General carpenter
notes: Canada Job Bank Muskoka-Kawarthas employment wages: $22–$48/hr. Contractor billing rate (labour-only to client) typically 2.5–3x employment wage in cottage-country market. Low end reflects smaller residential jobs; high end reflects premium finish work. Confirmed $50–$150/hr GC range in Bracebridge via HomeStars (https://www.homestars.com/home-constructions-renovations/general-contractor-pros/bracebridge). Muskoka construction costs run 20–40% above rural Ontario average per multiple Muskoka builder sources. Prices exclude HST.

name: Lead carpenter / journeyman
notes: Canada Job Bank Muskoka-Kawarthas median carpenter wage $32/hr; high $48/hr (Nov 2025 data). Journeyman billing rate to client typically 3–3.5x employment wage in premium cottage market. Canada Construction Network reports Ontario average carpenter at $31.80/hr employment wage (Mar 2026) at https://canadaconstructionnetwork.com/reports/contractor-wages-carpenter-rates-across-canada-2026. Vista Builders 2026 guide cites carpentry at $40–$75/hr billed — that is mainland Ontario baseline; Muskoka adds 20–30%. Prices exclude HST.

name: Apprentice carpenter
notes: Canada Job Bank Ontario apprentice carpenter: $22–$48/hr employment wage (Muskoka-Kawarthas same as provincial). Carpenters ICI Drywall Agreement 2025-2028 sets 1st-term apprentice at $28.67/hr (60% of journeyperson rate) — ICI union scale, higher than typical residential. Residential billing estimate for apprentice: 1.8–2.2x employment wage, reflecting supervision overhead in cottage-country market. Source also: https://www.isca.ca/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Carpenters-ICI-Drywall-Agreement-2025-2028.pdf. Prices exclude HST.

name: Licensed plumber
notes: PlumbWize Ontario 2026 guide: standard plumbing labour $110–$145/hr; Premier Plumbing Toronto range $110–$200/hr (https://premierplumbing.ca/plumbing-cost-guide/). Canada Job Bank Ontario median plumber wage $32.50/hr; top earners $50.38/hr per Herzing Canada (https://blog.herzing.ca/trades/how-much-do-plumbers-make-in-ontario-latest-salary-info). Muskoka premium +15–25% over base Ontario rural rates applies given travel, logistics, and limited local tradespeople. Service-call minimum: 1-hour charge, commonly $150–$200 call-out fee absorbed or billed at $150–$175 minimum on top of hourly. After-hours: $200–$350/hr. Prices exclude HST.

name: Licensed electrician
notes: Grizzli 2026 Ontario electrician guide: journeyman $80–$130/hr province-wide; Kitchener-Waterloo-Barrie region (closest proxy to Muskoka) $95–$140/hr. Muskoka/cottage-country premium estimated at +15–25% above Barrie-area baseline given travel and limited licensed contractors. Service-call minimum: typically 1-hour minimum; many Muskoka shops charge 2-hour minimum for off-site calls = $220–$350 minimum bill. Master electrician/owner-operator: $150–$200/hr province-wide. Sources also: https://padielectric.ca/how-much-do-residential-electricians-charge-near-me/ and ECRA/ESA licensing registry at https://www.esasafe.com. Prices exclude HST.

name: Drywaller — board + tape + mud + sand + ready-to-prime
notes: CSG Renovation Canada guide: full drywall installation (hanging + taping + finishing to Level 4/5) $1.50–$3.50/sqft nationally. Toronto professional rates: $1.75–$3.75/sqft for mud and tape alone (GMC Construction, https://gmco.ca/2025-drywall-costs-toronto/). Construct Estimates 2026: $1.50–$3.50/sqft. Muskoka premium +20–30% over Toronto/suburban baseline reflecting supply chain, travel, and cottage labour scarcity. Range shown is total installed (board + tape + mud + sand + ready-to-prime per task description). Level 4/5 finishes typical for Muskoka renovation clients. Prices exclude HST.

name: Painter — two-coat interior walls
notes: Pro Works Canada 2025: interior walls $1–$4/sqft; Absolute Home Services Toronto: $2–$3/sqft (https://www.absolutehomeservices.ca/blog/the-cost-of-painting-a-house-in-toronto-average-prices-factors-that-affect-them). InsideOut Painting 2025 data: $2–$6/sqft with $2.75/sqft average (https://www.insideoutpainting.ca/blog/how-much-does-it-cost-to-paint-the-interior-of-a-house-2025-data). Rates shown are LABOUR only for two-coat interior application; paint material is separate. Muskoka premium applies for travel and scheduling. High end reflects vaulted/cathedral ceilings, complex trim, and tight scheduling windows. Prices exclude HST.

name: Tile setter — standard floor/wall tile labour only
notes: Apollo Tile comprehensive guide: labour $4–$32/sqft depending on complexity and region. Elit Tile 2025: basic installs $4–$12/sqft; intricate patterns $8–$20/sqft. US-based benchmark but Canadian pricing within 10–15%. Ontario/Toronto range for standard tile (straight lay, bathroom/kitchen floor): $8–$15/sqft labour only. Muskoka premium +15–25% over Toronto suburban baseline. High end ($18) reflects herringbone, large-format (24×24+), or boat-access deliveries. Prices exclude HST.

name: Flooring installer — hardwood (nail-down/glue-down)
notes: Supreme Flooring Ontario 2026: labour $3–$6/sqft southern Ontario standard install; Big Bro Hardwood: nail-down $3.50–$6.50/sqft, glue-down $5.50–$9.50/sqft (https://bigbrohardwood.com/hardwood-floor-installation-cost-in-2025-price-per-square-foot-and-hidden-fees/). VC Floor Canada 2026: $4–$10/sqft labour average (https://vcfloor.ca/hardwood-flooring-installation-cost-in-canada/). Muskoka premium +20–25% over southern Ontario baseline. Labour only; materials separate. High end reflects exotic species, complex patterns, or access difficulty. Prices exclude HST.

name: Flooring installer — LVP / laminate (floating/click-lock)
notes: Parqueteam Toronto 2025: laminate installation $1.50–$2.50/sqft base Toronto labour. Pro Flooring Installers 2025: LVP/laminate $1.50–$3.00/sqft (https://www.proflooringinstallers.com/flooring-installation-cost-in-2025-complete-pricing-guide/). Muskoka premium +20–30% over Toronto suburban baseline — cottage scheduling, access, and travel justify upper range. LVP glue-down adds $1–$1.50/sqft. High end reflects difficult access (boat-in sites), removal of old flooring, or LVP glue-down method. Labour only; materials separate. Prices exclude HST.

name: HVAC technician — residential service/installation
notes: Indeed Canada April 2026: Ontario HVAC tech average employment wage $34.55/hr; GTA top $38.74/hr. Contractor billing rate to client: Fieldy 2026 guide $85–$150/hr residential (https://getfieldy.com/blogs/hvac-service-cost-pricing); Housecall Pro 2026: $75–$150/hr (https://www.housecallpro.com/resources/how-to-price-hvac-services/). Service call fee: $95–$220 standard diagnostic. Muskoka premium +20–30% over southern Ontario: limited local HVAC contractors, travel, seasonal demand spikes. High end for emergency/after-hours. Prices exclude HST.

name: Roofer — asphalt shingle labour only
notes: HomeGuide 2025-2026: shingle roof labour $200–$350 per square US national average; Canadian rates track ~15–30% higher due to WCB/WSIB, higher overhead. D'Angelo and Sons Ontario 2026: total installed $3.50–$5.50/sqft asphalt; labour component $150–$250/sqft (https://dangeloandsons.com/ontario-roof-replacement-cost-roof-type-prices/). Zekes Peaks Ontario roofer: $6–$7/sqft total all-in (materials+labour) for Ontario; implies labour-only ~$3–$4/sqft = $300–$400/square. Muskoka premium +15–25%: steep pitches common on cottage country builds, access logistics. All values per roofing square (100 sqft). Labour only; shingles, underlayment, and tear-off billed separately. Prices exclude HST.

name: Excavator operator — with machine
notes: Canada Job Bank Muskoka-Kawarthas excavator operator employment wage: $25.28–$44.83/hr (Nov 2025 data). Billing rate to client (operator + machine) is significantly higher: machine ownership/lease, insurance, mobilization, and overhead add $80–$140/hr on top of operator wage. AllTo Construction Ontario: $100–$160/hr for excavation labour (https://www.alltoconstruction.com/blog/how-much-does-a-septic-system-cost-in-ontario). Island Equipment Owners Association suggested minimum rates (BC-based): Class 3–5 machines $195–$219/hr machine-only (https://www.ieoa.ca/rates/). Muskoka rocky terrain/Canadian Shield: blasting and specialty equipment may add significant costs. Rate shown is operator + machine combined, standard residential-scale (10–20 tonne excavator). Low end reflects backhoe-scale, high end reflects larger machine or difficult rock site. Prices exclude HST.

name: Septic installer — conventional cottage system lump sum
notes: Bennett Inc. Ontario 2026: conventional gravity system $10,000–$20,000 good-access sites; advanced treatment $25,000–$50,000+; raised bed $20,000–$40,000 (https://bennettinc.ca). M. Riddle Excavating Ontario 2025: average $18,000–$35,000 (https://mriddleexcavating.ca/blog/septic-system-costs-in-ontario/). MyOwnCottage Muskoka 2026 guide: Muskoka septic systems start at $25,000 and rise with bedroom count and terrain (https://myowncottage.ca/cost-to-build-a-cottage-in-muskoka/). Jeffrey Braun Muskoka 2026: septic often starts $25,000+ with rocky terrain (https://www.jeffreybraun.ca/blog/99302/how-much-does-it-cost-to-build-a-cottage-in-muskoka). Low = conventional gravity system, good soil, road access, 3-bedroom cottage. Typical = mixed-difficulty Muskoka lot. High = advanced treatment (tertiary) or raised-bed system, poor soil/rock, boat-access site. Permit ($450), design ($1,000–$3,000), and soil testing ($500–$2,000) included in range. Prices exclude HST.

name: Boat-access cottage premium
notes: MyOwnCottage Muskoka 2026: island/boat-access premium 10%–25% over mainland equivalent. Jeffrey Braun Muskoka 2026: +15%–25% for boat-access builds due to barge transport and shorter working windows (https://www.jeffreybraun.ca/blog/99302/how-much-does-it-cost-to-build-a-cottage-in-muskoka). TechHome Ltd 2025: Muskoka construction 20–40% higher than rural Ontario overall (https://techhomeltd.com/2025/07/how-much-does-it-cost-to-build-a-custom-cottage-in-muskoka/). Add this % to ALL line items for boat-access jobs; barge mobilization, multiple transport runs, and weather delays justify the premium. Higher end (25%) for island sites with no road access at all. Prices exclude HST.

name: Winter / shoulder-season premium (Nov–Mar)
notes: Industry consensus: winter/off-season discounts of 5–15% are sometimes offered for interior work in slow periods; however in Muskoka the dynamic is reversed — winter ADDS cost due to heating, frozen ground, shorter days, and road-condition delays. House Remodel Cost guide documents 5–15% seasonal variance in either direction (https://houseremodelcost.com/blog/seasonal-renovation-costs-when-is-the-best-time-to-remodel). Muskoka-specific sources note frozen ground, reduced daylight, road-weight restrictions (spring), and limited subcontractor availability in off-season drive costs up, not down. No single published Muskoka-specific percentage found — this range is a professional estimate based on: (a) general seasonal labour premium literature, (b) Muskoka winter construction challenges documented by Ballantyne Builds (https://ballantynebuilds.com/what-does-it-really-cost-to-build-a-cottage-in-muskoka/). Flag as ESTIMATE — needs verification with local Muskoka contractors. Winter discount possible for purely interior, dry, heated work if contractor is hungry for work.

name: Travel surcharge — beyond Bracebridge service area
unit: per km (round trip) OR flat per day
notes: NJC (Government of Canada) kilometric rate for Ontario 2026: $0.62/km for first 4,000km (https://www.njc-cnm.gc.ca/directive/travel-voyage/td-dv-a2-eng.php). CRA mileage allowance is cost-floor; contractors bill higher to cover vehicle, overhead, and time. FieldCamp 2026 guide: contractors charge $0.65–$1.25/mile = $0.40–$0.78/km PLUS time; total effective rate $0.75–$1.50/km round-trip is common (https://fieldcamp.ai/blog/how-to-charge-for-travel-time/). Typical structure: no surcharge within 30km of shop (Bracebridge/Huntsville/Gravenhurst town centres); beyond 30km, per-km charge applies. Remote/boat-access: flat per-day travel charge of $150–$400/day plus equipment barge costs. Per-km rate shown is round-trip. No Muskoka-specific published rate found — estimate based on provincial and industry norms. Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — renovation (Bracebridge)
notes: Bracebridge By-law 2020-073 Schedule A: Repairs or Renovations = $11.30 per $1,000 of construction value; minimum $250. This is the building permit fee only; separate development charges apply for new construction (not renovations). Permit only; does not include development charges. Example: $100,000 kitchen/bath renovation = $1,130 permit fee. Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — new residential dwelling (Bracebridge)
notes: Bracebridge By-law 2020-073 Schedule A: Dwellings = $11.30 per $1,000 of construction value OR $1.50/sqft of finished floor area — whichever is greater; minimum $310. Development charges additional: $10,389/unit Town + $22,514/unit District = $32,903 combined for urban single-detached (2026) per Bracebridge Development Charges By-law 2024-034 (https://www.bracebridge.ca/town-services/building-services/building-permits/development-charges/). Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — renovation (Township of Muskoka Lakes)
notes: Muskoka Lakes Schedule H (effective March 12 2025): Repairs or Renovations = $11.00 per $1,000 of construction value; minimum $200. New dwelling = $11.00 per $1,000 or $1.55/sqft — whichever greater; minimum $200. Septic permit: $450 new system, $200 new tank. 50% surcharge if construction starts before permit issued. Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — renovation (Gravenhurst)
notes: Gravenhurst 2025 User Fees Schedule 1: Building Permit Fee Not Otherwise Covered = $11.25 per $1,000 of construction value; minimum $200. New residential finished floor area = $1.45/sqft. Accessory buildings = $0.75/sqft. Septic system = $450 new install. Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — renovation (Huntsville)
notes: Town of Huntsville building permits page states: $11.00 per $1,000 of estimated project value of construction, minimum $125. Source: https://www.huntsville.ca/business-development-environment/building-and-renovating/building-permits/. Development charges (new construction only, NOT renovations): single/semi $9,734/unit (2026) per https://www.huntsville.ca/home-property/development-charges/. Note: Huntsville fee schedule PDF not publicly linked on website; fee confirmed from page text. Prices exclude HST.

name: Building permit — Lake of Bays Township
notes: Lake of Bays building permit fees not explicitly published on main page; fee schedule referenced in Schedule G of Fees and Charges By-law. Based on regional consistency: Muskoka Lakes, Bracebridge, Gravenhurst, and Huntsville all use $11.00–$11.30 per $1,000 construction value formula. Lake of Bays assumed consistent — needs manual confirmation via https://www.lakeofbays.on.ca or direct contact with Township office at 705-635-2272. Minimum fee likely $200. Prices exclude HST. Note: needs manual lookup to confirm exact schedule.

name: Septic system permit (all Muskoka townships)
notes: Muskoka Lakes Schedule H: new sewage system permit $450; new tank only $200. Bracebridge By-law 2020-073: new septic flat rate $450. Gravenhurst 2025: new septic $450. Consistent across surveyed Muskoka townships. Does not include soil testing ($500–$2,000), system design ($1,000–$3,000), or conservation authority review fees. Prices exclude HST.

name: General Carpenter (per hour)
notes: Source: Job Bank Canada – Muskoka-Kawarthas Region. Job Bank Canada (Nov 2025 data): Muskoka-Kawarthas carpenter median $32/hr; low $22/hr; high $48/hr. Billing rate to client: add overhead/profit = est. $65–$85/hr billed

name: Lead Carpenter / Site Foreman (per hour)
notes: Source: Job Bank Canada – Muskoka-Kawarthas Region. Job Bank Canada high end $48/hr; lead/foreman typically at or above regional median. Billing rate to client: ~$85–$110/hr

name: Apprentice Carpenter (per hour)
notes: Source: Job Bank Canada – Muskoka-Kawarthas Region. Job Bank Canada low: $22/hr for Muskoka-Kawarthas. Indeed.com Muskoka Lakes confirms ~$31.68/hr average (all levels). Billing rate: ~$45–$60/hr

name: Licensed Plumber (per hour)
notes: Source: Job Bank Canada – Muskoka-Kawarthas Region. Job Bank Canada (Nov 2025): plumbing contractor Muskoka-Kawarthas median $53.43/hr; range $24.20–$65.48. Billing rate to client: ~$110–$160/hr in rural ON. Use $55 wage; $130 billed typical

name: HVAC Technician (per hour)
notes: Source: Job Bank Canada – Ontario / Konstruction Group. Job Bank Canada: HVAC mechanic Ontario median $37/hr; range $21–$58/hr (Nov 2025 data). Billing rate to client: ~$80–$120/hr
